review_reason: NoMaxOrder (ConstructiveQuotient M_0 h_mcs_0) is mathematically false for generic M_0. G-complete MCSs (where phi in M iff G(phi) in M) are consistent with all axioms but make the ConstructiveQuotient a singleton, violating NoMaxOrder. All three pure-syntax paths (A: enriched seed, B: Aut+(T), C: pruned fragment) are blocked. User must decide: (1) accept Path D bulldozing with Q import, (2) add non-G-complete hypothesis and restructure completeness, (3) construct M_0 from enriched seed {neg phi_0, F(G(neg phi_0))}, or (4) abandon ConstructiveQuotient approach.

artifacts.summary: Phase 2B blocked: NoMaxOrder false for generic M_0 due to G-complete MCSs
Plan v013 with Phase 2B marked BLOCKED and detailed progress notes

partial_progress.details: Phase 2B blocked: the statement NoMaxOrder (ConstructiveQuotient M_0 h_mcs_0) is false for G-complete M_0. All three pure-syntax paths exhausted.

errors.message: NoMaxOrder (ConstructiveQuotient M_0 h_mcs_0) is false when M_0 is G-complete. G-complete MCSs exist and are consistent with all axioms. The ConstructiveQuotient from a G-complete M_0 is a singleton.
errors.recommendation: Accept Path D (bulldozing with Q), or add non-G-complete hypothesis to M_0, or construct M_0 from enriched seed ensuring F(G(psi)) in M_0

completion_data.completion_summary: Phase 2B blocked. Proved that NoMaxOrder on ConstructiveQuotient is false for generic M_0 by showing G-complete MCSs exist and make the quotient a singleton. All three pure-syntax paths (enriched seed, Aut+(T), pruned fragment) exhausted.
